output.headline: Inflation jumps as tech faces new pressure points
output.summary: U.S. inflation posted its biggest monthly increase in nearly four years after war-driven gasoline and diesel prices surged, putting fresh pressure on consumers and Washington. In tech, a suspect was detained after an alleged Molotov attack on Sam Altman’s home and threats at OpenAI, while Anthropic’s new Mythos model is drawing cybersecurity concerns and Apple’s foldable iPhone has reportedly moved into a new pre-production testing stage.
output.why_it_matters: The morning’s mix of hotter prices, AI security worries, and major platform shifts points to fresh volatility for households, policymakers, and the tech industry.

output.headline: Inflation jumps as AI and consumer tech tensions build
output.summary: U.S. inflation rose at its fastest pace in nearly four years after a war-driven spike in gasoline and diesel prices, putting fresh pressure on households and the White House. In tech, security concerns swirled around Anthropic's new Mythos model after some cybersecurity experts warned about its implications, while OpenAI faced a separate shock when a suspect was arrested after allegedly attacking Sam Altman's home and threatening the company's headquarters. Apple, meanwhile, moved its foldable iPhone rumors closer to product reality as testing advanced.
output.why_it_matters: Energy costs, AI safety concerns, and big-platform product bets are all converging into economic and tech risk people will feel quickly.

output.lead: A war-driven spike in gasoline and diesel sent U.S. inflation to its biggest monthly jump in nearly four years, adding fresh pressure to consumers, markets, and Washington. In tech, AI safety fears intensified around Anthropic's new model, security concerns hit OpenAI after an attack on Sam Altman's home, and industry attention stayed on Xbox management criticism and Apple's foldable iPhone plans.
summary_points: March inflation accelerated sharply as fuel costs surged after the Iran war, complicating the economic and political outlook.
Anthropic's new Mythos model drew cybersecurity concern, highlighting rising anxiety over advanced AI capabilities and misuse risks.
A suspect was arrested after allegedly attacking Sam Altman's home and threatening OpenAI headquarters in San Francisco.
Former Bethesda executive criticism renewed scrutiny of Xbox stewardship, while Apple's foldable iPhone rumors advanced toward preproduction.

In technology, AI safety concerns, a security incident targeting OpenAI chief Sam Altman, renewed criticism of Xbox management, and fresh signs of Apple’s foldable iPhone progress underscored a sector balancing growth, risk, and product ambition.

output.lead: A fuel shock tied to the Iran war pushed U.S. inflation to its biggest jump in nearly four years, jolting the economic outlook and adding political pressure in Washington. Meanwhile, tech news centered on security and strategy, from an attack linked to OpenAI's CEO to fresh scrutiny of Anthropic's new model, Xbox management criticism, and renewed talk of Apple's foldable iPhone.
summary_points: March consumer prices surged as gasoline and diesel jumped sharply, producing the strongest U.S. inflation increase in almost four years.
A suspect was arrested after allegedly attacking Sam Altman's home and threatening OpenAI's San Francisco headquarters, underscoring AI security risks.
Anthropic's Mythos drew concern from cybersecurity experts, highlighting growing unease over advanced AI systems and their possible misuse.
Apple's foldable iPhone rumors intensified as testing advanced, while a former Bethesda executive criticized Xbox's handling of the game business.
